title: Flexbox Deep Dive
description: Master Flexbox for complex layouts – alignment, wrapping, and ordering.

slides.title: Flexbox Recap 📐
slides.content:
# Quick Review

Flexbox is a layout model that allows items to align and distribute space within a container. Key properties:

- `flexDirection`: `row` | `column` (default column in RN)
- `justifyContent`: alignment along main axis
- `alignItems`: alignment along cross axis
- `flexWrap`: `wrap` | `nowrap` (default nowrap)
- `alignContent`: aligns wrapped lines
- `flex`: how much an item should grow relative to siblings

slides.title: Flex Wrap and Align Content 🧶
slides.content:
## Wrapping Items

When `flexWrap: 'wrap'` is set, items can flow onto multiple lines. Then `alignContent` controls the alignment of those lines (like `justifyContent` but for lines).

```jsx
<View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', flexWrap: 'wrap', alignContent: 'space-between' }}>
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }} />
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }} />
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }} />
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }} />
</View>
```

Common `alignContent` values: `flex-start`, `center`, `flex-end`, `stretch`, `space-between`, `space-around`.

slides.title: Flex Grow, Shrink, and Basis
slides.content:
## `flex` is shorthand

In React Native, `flex` is actually a combination of `flexGrow`, `flexShrink`, and `flexBasis`.

- `flexGrow`: how much the item grows relative to others (0 by default).
- `flexShrink`: how much it shrinks if needed (1 by default).
- `flexBasis`: the default size before growing/shrinking (auto by default).

Setting `flex: 1` is equivalent to `flexGrow: 1, flexShrink: 1, flexBasis: 0`.

```jsx
<View style={{ flex: 1 }}> {/* takes all remaining space */}
<View style={{ flexGrow: 1 }}> {/* grows but respects content size */}
```

quizData.question: What does `alignContent` control?

options.text: Alignment of individual items within a line
Alignment of wrapped lines as a whole

quizData.explanation: `alignContent` aligns the lines when wrapping is enabled, like `justifyContent` but for multiple lines.

slides.title: Absolute and Relative Positioning 📍
slides.content:
## Position in Flexbox

By default, all views have `position: 'relative'`. You can set `position: 'absolute'` to take an item out of the flow.

With `absolute`, you can use `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right` to position relative to the parent.

```jsx
<View style={{ flex: 1, backgroundColor: 'lightblue' }}>
  <View style={{
    position: 'absolute',
    top: 20,
    left: 20,
    width: 50,
    height: 50,
    backgroundColor: 'red'
  }} />
</View>
```

Absolute positioning ignores flexbox alignment.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use the React Native debugger or the built‑in Inspector to visualise Flexbox layouts. Shake your device → 'Toggle Inspector' and tap on any element to see its flex properties.
